mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate reduces the discount rate applied to future equity cash flows, mechanically boosting present valuations and driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate rises above 9.5%, it signals deteriorating labor market conditions that compress consumer spending and corporate earnings, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day forward returns in > 60% of triggered instances despite the unemployment threshold breach.

mechanism_narrative: A simultaneous contraction in industrial production and a decline in real personal savings rates indicate a supply-demand mismatch that erodes profit margins, driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: Forward returns do not exhibit a statistically significant negative skew when both INDPRO and RSAFS decline concurrently over a 3-month window.

mechanism_narrative: In a high-rate environment, rising housing starts amplify the wealth effect on consumer demand, supporting equity valuations and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The positive correlation between HOUST and SPX forward returns vanishes or turns negative when FEDFUNDS remains above 0.25% for consecutive quarters.

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly growth in real GDP directly proxies for aggregate economic expansion, which increases corporate revenue streams and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: Rank IC between quarterly GDPC1 growth and 60-day forward SPX returns falls below 0.05 across three consecutive rolling 5-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: When the CPI index breaches 220, it signals entrenched inflation that forces the Federal Reserve to maintain restrictive policy, compressing equity multiples and producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX forward returns remain positive or neutral in > 50% of observations where CPIAUCSL crosses the 220 threshold.

mechanism_narrative: Strong employment growth paired with rising real savings rates indicates robust household balance sheets that sustain consumption, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 20-day horizon.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict positive returns when PAYEMS and RSAFS rise concurrently, showing no predictive power over a 1-year backtest.

mechanism_narrative: During periods of constrained industrial output, rising housing starts act as a leading indicator of supply chain normalization, supporting corporate profitability and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward returns do not exhibit a positive skew when HOUST rises while INDPRO remains below 94.0, indicating the supply-demand linkage is broken.
